title: Ven Pongal (Savory South Indian Rice & Lentil Porridge)

ingredients.title: For Pongal
items: Rice – 1 cup (preferably Sona Masoori or Ponni)
Moong dal (yellow split gram) – ¼ cup
Ginger – 1 tsp, finely chopped or grated
Optional: a pinch of hing (asafoetida) for authentic flavor

instruction.title: Prep Rice & Dal
steps: Wash 1 cup rice and ¼ cup moong dal thoroughly.
Soak moong dal for 15–20 minutes (optional but makes it creamier).

instruction.title: Cooking Pongal
steps: In a heavy-bottomed pan or pressure cooker, dry roast moong dal lightly until aromatic (optional).
Add rice, dal, 4 cups water, salt, ginger, green chilies, black pepper, curry leaves, and hing.
3–4 whistles on medium heat (or simmer in pan until rice and dal are soft and mushy).
Once cooked, mash slightly with the back of a ladle to get a creamy texture.

instruction.title: Fry Cashews
steps: In a small pan, heat 3–4 tsp ghee.
Fry cashews until golden brown, remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Tempering (Tadka)
steps: In the same ghee, add ½ tsp mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add curry leaves and fried cashews.
Pour this tempering over the cooked Pongal.

instruction.title: Finishing Touch
steps: Drizzle extra ghee (1 tsp) on top for that rich, restaurant-quality shine.
Serve hot with Coconut Chutney and Sambar.

chefTips: Consistency: Pongal should be soft, slightly loose, but not watery. Think creamy porridge.
Freshly cracked black pepper & ginger enhance aroma and warmth.
Ghee is the secret for richness—don’t skimp.
Cashews should be golden and crisp, not burnt.
Optional: Add a tiny dollop of butter on top before serving for luxury finish.
